betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Advanced Particle Accelerator§f

§aMaster the PA mechanics — build linear or circular accelerators to produce exotic particles for endgame materials!§f

The §bParticle Accelerator§f is a complex modular machine that accelerates particles to relativistic speeds via superconducting magnets. Unlike the cyclotron which bombards elements to transmute them, the particle accelerator collides particles together to create new byproducts.

§6Types§f:
§b• Linear Accelerator§f — straight design, minimum 3 RF cavities + 3 quadrupole magnets
§b• Synchronous Accelerator§f — square design, needs dipole magnets for turns, can be scaled larger

§6Components§f:
§b• Beamline§f — connects different accelerator parts; allows cheap extension of ring size
  Recipe: Cast Steel Plate×8 + Copper Plate×16 + Dense Gold Wire×4
§b• Particle Source§f — creates particles from 2 input items; requires cooling
  Recipe: Beamline×3 + Cast Steel Plate×16 + PVC Bar×16 + Magnetron×16 + Quantum Processing Unit
§b• RF Cavity§f — accelerates particle by §e100 points§f, adds §e100 defocus§f; requires cooling
  Recipe: Beamline×3 + Cast Steel Plate×16 + Copper Plate×64 + PVC Bar×16 + Magnetron×16
§b• Quadrupole Magnets§f — reduces defocus by §e100 points§f; needs large coils; requires cooling
  Recipe: Beamline + Cast Steel Plate×16 + PVC Bar×16 + Versatile Circuit Board
§b• Dipole Magnets§f — redirects particles based on momentum; 3 output modes (below/above threshold + redstone); requires cooling
  Recipe: Beamline×2 + Cast Steel Plate×16 + PVC Bar×32 + Versatile Circuit Board×4
§b• Particle Detector§f — accepts particles and performs the recipe; needs defocus of 0; requires cooling
  Recipe: Beamline×3 + Cast Steel Plate×24 + Dense Gold Wire×16 + PVC Bar×16 + Quantum Processing Unit×4

§6Coil Types§f:
§b• Large Gold Coil§f — range §e0–2,200§f, dipole min side §e15§f; cheapest
§b• Large NbTi Coil§f — range §e1,500–8,400§f, dipole min §e21§f
§b• Large BSCCO Coil§f — range §e7,500–15,000§f, dipole min §e27§f
§b• Large Chlorophyte Coil§f — range §e14,500–75,000§f, dipole min §e51§f; best

§6Key Particle Recipes§f:
§b• Antimatter Capsule§f — Hydrogen Ion + Copper Ion @ §e300 momentum§f
§b• Antischrabidium Capsule§f — Antimatter×2 @ §e400§f
§b• Muon Capsule§f — Antimatter + Hydrogen Ion @ §e2,500§f
§b• Higgs Boson Capsule§f — Lead Ion + Hydrogen Ion @ §e6,500§f
§b• Dark Matter Capsule§f — Antischrabidium×2 @ §e10,000§f
§b• Strange Quark Capsule§f — Dark Matter + Muon @ §e12,500§f
§b• Sparkticle Capsule§f — Pulverized Enchantment + Strange Quark @ §e12,500§f
§b• Digamma Particle§f — Higgs Boson + Sparkticle @ §e70,000§f

§cWarning§f: Particles crash if defocus reaches §e1,000§f. Ensure enough quadrupole magnets per RF cavity. Coils below minimum range cause §e10×§f power penalty. Multiple penalties stack up to §e100×§f power draw, exceeding the magnet's power buffer and crashing the particle.

§eTip§f: Start with a linear accelerator for basic particles (antimatter, higgs boson). Build a synchronous (square) accelerator for higher-momentum particles like sparkticles and the digamma particle. The PA requires several MHE to run — ensure you have adequate power!
